The '''Cor''' were a [[Feline/Legends|feline]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the jungle [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Gargolyn IV]]. The planet was chosen for settlement by the [[Karflo Corporation/Legends|Karflo Corporation]], which tried to forcibly relocate a Cor tribe despite legends warning of peril if this occurred. The following day, a large [[Gargolyn monster|monster]] erupted from the ground, destroying Karflo's chosen site. Only the return of the Cor tribe was able to subdue the beast.

==Biology and appearance==
The Cor were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Feline/Legends|felines]]. They were small in stature, growing to about one [[Meter/Legends|meter]] in height.<ref name="SWGS" /> They had a long, thin tail, and four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] that each ended in five sharp claws. Each Cor had a tapered snout and long pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. They were covered in fur except for their tail, which appeared bald.<ref name="SWGS" />

==Society and culture==
The Cor were a primitive people. They had traditional tribal lands in which they lived together in villages. Their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Gargolyn IV, had no native predators, and the Cor themselves were a non-violent people, preferring to use peaceful protest, exemplified when the [[Karflo Corporation/Legends|Karflo Corporation]] tried to forcibly relocate one group from their [[Cor village|village]].<ref name="SWGS" />

Although unaware of the existence of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], members of the species were in fact [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and attuned to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]. They had [[Shaman/Legends|shamans]] who utilized this affinity.<ref name="SWGS" />

==History==
[[File:Karflo_Corporation_logo_vector.svg|thumb|left|The Karflo Corporation tried to colonize Gargolyn IV.]]
The Cor evolved on Gargolyn IV,<ref name="SWGS" /> a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> where they achieved sentience and created a primitive society. In the dim past, one particular Cor tribe was frequently attacked by a [[Gargolyn monster|monster]] that killed many who tried to stop it. Eventually, a shaman managed to subdue the beast, binding it to the land. A legend developed that so long as the shaman's people lived on that spot, the beast would remain bound and captive.<ref name="SWGS" />

Some time later, the planet was scouted by the Karflo Corporation,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|323|Gargolyn IV}}</ref> who decided to establish a colony on Gargolyn IV. They sought settlers to built a temporary [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] and provide other aid to the project. Karflo also wanted to build a manufacturing facility on Gargolyn, with a Cor village chosen as the ideal site for the construction. That necessitated relocating the Cor tribe, which refused to budge. Karflo authorized force to move them, despite legends warning against any such relocation.<ref name="SWGS" />

A day after the tribe's eviction, a significant explosion erupted at the factory site, and a gigantic beast emerged.<ref name="SWGS" /> It utterly destroyed any settlement Karflo tried to build there.<ref name="CSWE" /> The settlers were forced to venture into Gargolyn's jungles to track down the displaced Cor tribe and return them to their ancestral lands; only in this manner, was the beast able to be subdued.<ref name="SWGS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Cor were created by [[Bill Olmesdahl]] for the ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen]]'', a [[West End Games]] product for the second edition of their role-playing game. Published in [[1992]], the book contains a number of adventure hooks to be fleshed out by game masters. The Cor adventure is titled "[[A Perfect World]]." In addition to the main proposed storyline, Olmesdahl offers alternative versions: one, where the beast is actually under the control of the Cor, and another where an agent for a rival corporation is trying to sabotage Karflo's colonization efforts.<ref name="SWGS" /> The adventure hook is alluded to in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]], in its entry for Gargolyn IV.<ref name="CSWE" />
